claim: Config resolve MUST select the binding whose tag set is the most specific subset of the request tags (largest matching tag count wins).
Config resolve MUST treat a binding as a candidate only when every binding tag is present in the request tag set (subset match, not exact equality).
quote:
var candidates = bindings
			.Where(b => !b.IsDeleted && PathMatches(b.Path, path))
			.Select(b => (Binding: b, Tags: ParseTags(b.Tags)))
			.Where(c => c.Tags.IsSubsetOf(requestSet))
			.ToList();

claim: When two or more equally specific bindings compete for the same path, resolve MUST fail loudly with AmbiguousConfigException rather than picking one silently.
The bulk resolve API MUST surface ambiguous path collisions as HTTP 409 with the colliding binding ids.
Soft-deleted config bindings MUST be excluded from resolve (delete is soft; resolve filters IsDeleted).
quote:
// Soft-delete: mark IsDeleted=1, keep the row. Resolve filters it out.
	// UI's history page can offer "Undelete" for the last deleted version.

claim: Config path matching MUST be case-insensitive and exact (no prefix/glob matching of paths).
quote:
static bool PathMatches(string bindingPath, string requestPath) =>
		string.Equals(bindingPath, requestPath, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

claim: Config tag comparison MUST be case-insensitive.
quote: var requestSet = new HashSet<string>(requestTags, StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase);

claim: The canonical config read surface MUST be GET /v1/conf (yobaconf-compatible bulk resolve) requiring config:read.
Bulk resolve MUST auto-inject workspace and project tags (ws:{workspaceKey}, project:{projectKey}) derived from the calling API key's project claim.
Resolved config responses MUST support ETag / If-None-Match and return 304 when the resolved set is unchanged.
Config resolve MUST offer response templates flat, dotnet, envvar, and envvar-deep (plus dotenv as text/plain) so consumers can consume without bespoke reshaping.
The dotenv template MUST return text/plain KEY=value lines so standard tooling (docker --env-file, compose env_file, shell source) can consume config with zero PetBox client.
Secret bindings MUST be decryptable at resolve time via AES-GCM when PETBOX_MASTER_KEY is configured; missing master key makes secret encryption unavailable.
Config bindings MUST support BindingKind Plain vs Secret; secret values are stored with Ciphertext/Iv/AuthTag fields for at-rest protection.
On resolve, Secret bindings MUST be decrypted when the encryptor is available and ciphertext material is present; otherwise the stored Value is returned.
Config write APIs MUST authorize the target workspace from the caller's project claim (or '*' cross-project key), not trust the workspaceKey route segment alone.
Creating a binding MUST require tags to include the workspace tag ws:{workspaceKey}.
quote:
if (!dto.Tags.Contains($"ws:{workspaceKey}", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
			return Results.BadRequest(new ErrorResponse($"Tags must include 'ws:{workspaceKey}'"));

claim: Config storage MUST be workspace-scoped (one config SQLite DB per workspace).
Config schema evolution MUST be additive (never drop columns); bootstrap is idempotent per workspace file.
Bulk resolve MUST resolve every distinct non-deleted path present in the binding set against the request tag vector.
Data module MUST expose per-(project, name) SQLite databases with lifecycle endpoints for create, list, and delete.
DataDb create/delete MUST require data:schema scope; listing DataDbs MUST require data:read.
quote:
// All endpoints require `data:schema` scope EXCEPT GET which uses `data:read`
// (listing is harmless reconnaissance).

claim: Data access MUST prevent cross-project reach: the API key project claim MUST authorize the URL projectKey (including '*' for cross-project keys).
PetBox MUST provide a raw-SQL pass-through: POST query (data:read) returns rows; POST exec (data:write) returns affected count.
Scopes data:read, data:write, and data:schema MUST partition Data capabilities (list/query vs row writes vs schema apply and DataDb lifecycle).
quote:
new(DataRead,    "Read DataDbs",           "List DataDbs, SELECT via /api/data/{p}/{db}/query, describe schemas.",       "Data"),
		new(DataWrite,   "Write DataDb rows",      "INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE via /api/data/{p}/{db}/exec.",                          "Data"),
		new(DataSchema,  "Schema apply / lifecycle","CREATE/DROP tables, apply DbUp migrations, create/delete DataDbs.",          "Data"),

claim: REST and MCP data SQL execution MUST share one IDataSqlService so security rules (PRAGMA deny-list, timeouts, SQLITE_FULL mapping) cannot drift between adapters.
Exec MUST deny a small PRAGMA deny-list (writable_schema, temp_store_directory, data_store_directory, trusted_schema) while default-allowing other SQL to preserve the raw-SQL pass-through promise.
Query/exec CommandTimeout MUST default to 30s and MAY be overridden per request via X-PetBox-Timeout-Seconds, capped at 300s.
quote:
// CommandTimeout defaults to 30s. Override per-request via header
// `X-PetBox-Timeout-Seconds` (capped at 300s).

claim: SQLITE_FULL on query/exec MUST map to HTTP 507; other SQLite errors MUST map to HTTP 400 with the raw message and code.
Schema apply MUST be hash-idempotent: same name+hash is AlreadyApplied (no-op); same name with different hash is Conflict (HTTP 409).
Each DataDb MUST own its own migration journal table (__SchemaVersions) inside that DataDb file, not in the main PetBoxDb.
quote:
// The journal table name (`__SchemaVersions`) lives inside the DataDb file
// itself, not in main PetBoxDb. Each DataDb owns its own migration history.

claim: Schema apply MUST require data:schema; listing applied migrations MUST require data:read and expose the journal via a stable endpoint shape (not raw table coupling).
PetBox MUST NOT own pet table schemas at DataDb create time; pets apply schema via the /schema endpoint (DbUp-style named scripts).
New DataDbs MUST be created with WAL journal mode and a persistent max_page_count quota (INSERT over the limit → SQLITE_FULL).
DataDb maxPageCount MUST default to 262144 (~1 GB at 4 KB pages) and MUST reject values below 1024 (4 MB floor).
A hosted WAL checkpoint service MUST periodically run PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) on every active DataDb so WAL sidecars stay bounded under per-request connections.
DataDb delete MUST remove the metadata row immediately and treat on-disk file removal as best-effort; locked files MUST be cleaned later by orphan cleanup.
An orphan cleanup service MUST eventually delete on-disk DataDb files (and .wal/.shm sidecars) that have no corresponding DataDbs metadata row.
DataDb names MUST match ^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,99}$ and MUST NOT use reserved names such as __schema_versions.
Query/exec request body size MUST be bounded (query 1 MB, exec 10 MB) to prevent oversized JSON from OOMing the server.
Data SQL execution MUST open a fresh connection per request (connection strings only; no long-lived pooled command ownership at the factory layer).
Config write MUST support soft-delete of a binding by exact (path, tags) identity while retaining the row for history/undelete.
Creating a DataDb MUST require the project to exist in PetBoxDb.Projects (PetBox is source of truth for project identity).
Ambiguous config error responses MUST include the path and candidate binding ids so callers can disambiguate.
Denied PRAGMA on exec MUST fail closed with a clear error naming the forbidden pragma (REST maps to HTTP 400).
Orphan cleanup and WAL checkpoint MUST be BackgroundServices that reconcile eventually without request-path coordination (survive restarts; next tick fixes disk state).
Missing DataDb for query/exec/schema MUST return not-found (DataDbNotFoundException → REST 404).
Config clients targeting /v1/conf MUST be able to pass additional request tags as query parameters (key:value) alongside auto-injected ws/project tags.
Config resolve is pure tag-based matching — no FK joins to Project/Service entities; same tags MUST yield the same winner (deterministic most-specific-set).
Schema apply conflict responses MUST return both existingHash and providedHash so pets can detect content drift of a named migration.
quote: public sealed record SchemaApplyResponse(string Kind, string Hash, string? ExistingHash);

claim: Product MUST gate each subsystem behind a feature flag read from configuration, such that a disabled feature registers no module endpoints or runtime wiring for that subsystem.
Product MUST support a closed set of feature modules: Config, Logging, Data, Dashboard, Tasks, Memory, LlmRouter, and Deploy.
quote:
public enum Feature
{
	Config,
	Logging,
	Data,
	Dashboard,
	Tasks,
	Memory,
	LlmRouter,
	Deploy,
}

claim: Product MUST only register a module's services and endpoints when that module's feature flag is enabled.
Product MUST authenticate API keys against a local catalog by default, with Auth mode configurable as local or remote.
Product MUST, when Auth mode is remote, validate presented API keys by calling a central instance's /api/auth/validate endpoint and treating non-success as invalid.
Product MUST expose GET /api/auth/validate that returns the authenticated key's project claim and scopes on success, or valid:false with 401 when unauthenticated.
Product MUST accept API keys only from an enumerated catalog of scopes; any scope string not in the catalog MUST be rejected as invalid.
Product MUST treat ApiKey scopes as explicit enumerated permissions (config/logs/health/data/tasks/memory/llm/deploy/agent/agents/admin families), never as free-form wildcard scope strings.

Product MUST separate methodology approval capability (tasks:approve) from ordinary tasks write access; approval is the maintainer capability for enforceApproval-gated transitions and is meaningless without tasks:write.
quote: new(TasksApprove, "Approve tasks",         "Perform approval-gated workflow transitions the methodology ENFORCES (enforceApproval) — the maintainer capability. Meaningless without tasks:write.", "Tasks"),

claim: Product MUST treat admin:provision as root-equivalent: it may mint API keys with any scopes for any project (including admin:provision itself), create projects, and set config bindings.
quote: new(AdminProvision, "Provision projects & keys", "ROOT-EQUIVALENT: mints API keys with ANY scopes for ANY project (including admin:provision itself), creates projects (project.*), sets config bindings (config.*). Issue deliberately; prefer short-lived keys for routine work.", "Admin"),

claim: Product MUST allow an API key's project claim to be the wildcard "*", authorizing access to any requested projectKey, while tools still operate under the real requested projectKey so "*" never becomes a storage path.
Product MUST accept API keys via X-Api-Key, legacy X-YobaConf-ApiKey, or Authorization Bearer/Token schemes, with X-Api-Key taking precedence.
Product MUST reject an API key once its optional ExpiresAt instant has passed (null means never expires).
Product MUST prefer config-declared API keys over DB-minted keys on collision so bootstrap/CI keys cannot be silently overridden by a UI-minted key with the same secret.
Product MUST support config-sourced API keys that cannot be revoked through the UI; their lifecycle is owned by the configuration source.
Product MUST model human workspace membership with roles Admin, Member, and Viewer.
Product MUST treat a sysadmin principal as satisfying any workspace role requirement across every workspace.
Product MUST allow workspace administration only to a sysadmin or a holder of the Admin role for that specific workspace.
Product MUST seed an env-configured admin account only on first boot while no $system administrator exists, and MUST NOT re-create or refresh that account once a $system admin exists.
Product MUST auto-seed a built-in $system workspace and $system project on initial migration.
quote:
Execute.Sql("INSERT INTO Workspaces (Key, Name, Description, CreatedAt) VALUES ('$system', 'System', 'Built-in system workspace', datetime('now'))");

		Create.Table("Projects")
			.WithColumn("Key").AsString(100).PrimaryKey().NotNullable()
			.WithColumn("WorkspaceKey").AsString(100).NotNullable()
			.WithColumn("Name").AsString(200).NotNullable()
			.WithColumn("Description").AsString(1000).Nullable();

		Create.Table("Services")
			.WithColumn("Key").AsString(100).PrimaryKey().NotNullable()
			.WithColumn("ProjectKey").AsString(100).NotNullable()
			.WithColumn("HealthModel").AsInt32().NotNullable()
			.WithColumn("Url").AsString(500).Nullable()
			.WithColumn("Version").AsString(50).Nullable()
			.WithColumn("ShortSha").AsString(8).Nullable()
			.WithColumn("Health").AsInt32().NotNullable().WithDefaultValue(3)
			.WithColumn("CheckedAt").AsDateTime().Nullable();

		Create.Table("ApiKeys")
			.WithColumn("Key").AsString(100).PrimaryKey().NotNullable()
			.WithColumn("ProjectKey").AsString(100).NotNullable()
			.WithColumn("Scopes").AsString(int.MaxValue).NotNullable()
			.WithColumn("CreatedAt").AsDateTime().NotNullable();

		Insert.IntoTable("Projects").Row(new { Key = "$system", WorkspaceKey = "$system", Name = "System", Description = "Built-in system project" });

claim: Product MUST refuse deletion of reserved built-in projects $system and $workspace.
Product MUST provide a reserved $workspace project under the $system workspace as the built-in container for cross-project shared memory.
quote:
// row exists, so the reserved container needs a Projects row like "$system" does. It
// lives under the "$system" workspace; it is a memory container, not a user project.

claim: Product MUST organize tenancy as Workspace (top-level container) containing Projects (Key, WorkspaceKey, Name, Description).
Product MUST store an API key with project claim, scopes, optional name, creation time, and optional expiry.
Product MUST model ConfigBindings as path/value/tags records with Plain or Secret kind, soft-delete, and version/content-hash metadata — not foreign-keyed to a project.

Product MUST cascade-delete a project's owned Core-DB bookkeeping rows (keys, health endpoints, data dbs/tables, queries, share links, logs meta, task boards meta, memory stores meta, relations, agent definitions, project settings) when the project is deleted, while NOT cascading tag-based ConfigBindings or HealthReports.
Product MUST expose a uniform entity-family read contract where list is search without a query and relevance sort exists only with a query.
Product MUST return search responses with optional response-budget markers (Truncated/Omitted/Hint) and retriever provenance (which retrievers ran / degraded).
Product MUST bound list-shaped agent tool results by a response-wide character budget and MUST NOT truncate silently — cuts are marked with truncated, omitted count, and a narrowing hint.

Product MUST provide a single search facade that addresses documents by entity (scope, type, id), routes synchronous writes inside the entity transaction, and fuses multi-index reads with honest degradation provenance.
Product MUST keep the lexical search floor transactionally consistent with entity writes (Synchronous class), while vector/enrichment indexes may be Eventual and best-effort.
Product MUST surface search degradation honestly when a requested retriever fails or is unavailable, rather than silently returning partial results.
Product MUST support multilingual lexical search: FTS query tokenization MUST retain non-ASCII Unicode letters/digits so non-Latin queries are not silently emptied.
Product MUST never block entity write paths on embedding/enrichment; Class-B indexes are asynchronous delta subscribers over the temporal version log.
Product MUST provide optimistic-concurrency temporal upserts where each desired row carries a watermark baseline version; any genuine conflict aborts the whole batch and returns conflicts for rebase.
Product MUST return, on temporal upsert, a sinceVersion delta of Added/Updated/Removed active rows plus CurrentVersion so callers advance a single cursor without re-reading.

Product MUST auto-resolve bookkeeping-only concurrent rewrites (payload unchanged since baseline, including A→B→A) instead of raising Stale, and MUST report those keys in AutoResolved so resolution is never silent.
Product MUST keep Class-A (lexical) search index updates inside the temporal apply transaction so a committed entity is never lexically stale and a throw rolls both back together.
Product MUST store IsSecret settings as encrypted ciphertext (cipher+iv+tag) and render them as password inputs with reveal.
Product MUST track task boards as explicit metadata (no auto-vivify), with plan nodes stored in a separate per-board temporal database file.
Product MUST allow closing/archiving a task board: a closed board rejects writes but stays readable with history retained.
Product MUST let a work board declare its linked SpecBoard name so work→spec task_spec targets are validated against that board rather than guessed among several.
Product MUST store directed Relations by stable NodeId (not mutable Key) so edges survive renames, with soft-close history via ClosedAt.

Product MUST store portable agent-definition documents server-side as roster-only data: role→model binding and owner ($HOME) axis are local, not server columns; any "model" property in the JSON tree MUST be rejected.
Product MUST periodically snapshot every internal SQLite database under the data directory into retained point-in-time backup sets (default interval 12h, retain 14 sets).
Product MUST authorize API-key actions by requiring an exact scope from the key's comma-separated scopes claim.
Product MUST never orphan-sweep reserved built-in project file scopes ($system / $workspace); their files must survive project-deletion cleanup.
quote: // Reserved built-ins ($system / $workspace) are never swept: their files must survive

claim: Product MUST classify FutureBaseline when a write quotes a version the scope never reached (typically a cursor carried over from a different board/store).
Product MUST soft-delete temporal rows by closing the active revision with no replacement, exposing deletes in the sinceVersion Removed set, with version 0 meaning delete current active regardless.
Product MUST separate portable agent-definition scopes (agents:read/write) from deploy node-agent poll/heartbeat scopes (agent:poll/heartbeat).
Product MUST track memory stores as explicit metadata (no auto-vivify), with entries in a separate per-store temporal database file.
Product MUST report Stale temporal conflicts with entity-scoped ChangedFields so retries are informed, not blind.
Product MUST model Workspace as a top-level organizational container with Key, Name, Description, and CreatedAt.
MCP tools/list MUST trim the visible tool set to modules for which the caller's ApiKey holds any matching scope, while still failing open when scopes are missing or classification is unknown
MCP tool-call security MUST be enforced at call time via AssertScope/AssertProject (and feature asserts); tools/list filtering MUST NOT be treated as the authorization boundary
quote:
// NOT a security boundary: every tool still enforces its exact scope at call time
// via ModuleMcp.AssertScope/AssertProject — this only shortens the listing.

claim: MCP tools/list SHOULD compact heavy tool descriptions to a head above a [[full]] sentinel, keeping full prose fetchable via tool_describe without mutating the server's canonical ToolCollection
tool_describe MUST return the full description plus input/output JSON schemas for a tool named exactly as in tools/list
quote: [Description("Return the FULL description of a tool by name. tools/list serves a COMPACT head for heavy tools (purpose + critical gotchas); call this to fetch the complete prose (all sections, sentinel merged out) plus the tool's input/output JSON schema. Pass `name` exactly as it appears in tools/list (e.g. tasks_upsert).")]

claim: Every MCP tool exception MUST be converted to a structured learning error envelope { error: { type, message, detail } } on the text content channel with IsError=true and no structuredContent
MCP success results that declare an outputSchema MUST return structuredContent conforming to that schema; error results MUST NOT pretend success by shipping non-conforming structuredContent
Generated MCP output schemas MUST treat nullable record properties as non-required so null-omitting serialization (WhenWritingNull / bodyLen) stays conformant for strict clients
whoami MUST be callable by any authenticated ApiKey with no scope requirement and MUST return the key's project claim and granted scopes
report_issue MUST accept any authenticated key (not project- or scope-gated beyond /mcp auth) and MUST file into fixed $system board client-issues for maintainer triage

report_issue SHOULD be used for systemic PetBox friction (repeated tool failures, misleading docs, confusing response shapes, missing capabilities), not one-off noise or the caller's own project workflow issues
quote:
File a bug / issue / feedback about PetBox ITSELF — a tool misbehaved, a response is
		confusing/opaque, something is broken. The report goes to the PetBox maintainer's triage
		board, not your project. Friction with your OWN project's code or workflow is not an
		issue here — remember it with memory_remember instead.

		Report SYSTEMIC friction, not one-off noise.

claim: MCP tool names MUST use underscore module_verb naming (e.g. tasks_upsert, llm_chat, deploy_node_list, report_issue, whoami) as the stable wire identity agents pass to tools/list and tool_describe
quote: [Description("Return the FULL description of a tool by name. tools/list serves a COMPACT head for heavy tools (purpose + critical gotchas); call this to fetch the complete prose (all sections, sentinel merged out) plus the tool's input/output JSON schema. Pass `name` exactly as it appears in tools/list (e.g. tasks_upsert).")]

claim: MCP body-carrying surfaces MUST share one bodyLen contract: omitted uses the surface default; -1 full body; 0 omit body; N>0 first N chars with ellipsis when truncated
When projectKey is optional on an MCP tool, the server MUST default it to the key's single-project claim and MUST require an explicit projectKey for cross-project (*) keys
The MCP HTTP endpoint MUST be authenticated with the same ApiKey scheme as REST (/mcp + X-Api-Key) and MUST reject unauthenticated callers
MCP transport MUST use HTTP with Stateless=true so agents can reach PetBox at /mcp without sticky sessions

tools/list scope classification MUST map tool-name prefixes to modules (tasks_/session_→tasks, memory_→memory, log_→logs, data_/db_→data, deploy_→deploy, agent_def_→agents, config_→admin:provision) and leave unclassified tools shown
quote:
static string? ModuleOf(string tool) =>
		tool.StartsWith("tasks_", StringComparison.Ordinal) || tool.StartsWith("session_", StringComparison.Ordinal) ? "tasks"
		: tool.StartsWith("memory_", StringComparison.Ordinal) ? "memory"
		: tool.StartsWith("log_", StringComparison.Ordinal) ? "logs"
		: tool.StartsWith("data_", StringComparison.Ordinal) || tool.StartsWith("db_", StringComparison.Ordinal) ? "data"
		: tool.StartsWith("deploy_", StringComparison.Ordinal) ? "deploy"
		: tool.StartsWith("agent_def_", StringComparison.Ordinal) ? "agents"
		: tool.StartsWith("config_", StringComparison.Ordinal) ? ApiKeyScopes.AdminProvision
		: null; // project_* / apikey_* — provisioning-mixed (admin:provision shows ALL anyway), leave shown

claim: ILlmClient MUST expose project-scoped Embed, Rerank, Chat, and a non-blocking IsAvailable probe so consumers depend only on the neutral contract, never the router implementation
LLM router capabilities MUST include Embed, Rerank, and Chat as first-class capability kinds with per-capability ordered provider chains

Capability routing MUST walk a project's priority-ordered route chain, skip circuit-open endpoints without connecting, fall through on transient upstream failures, and surface non-transient 4xx immediately without masking
A route with null tier MUST serve any requested tier; a tiered route MUST only match its exact tier, with priority deciding order among matches
IsAvailableAsync MUST report whether any configured provider for a capability is present and not circuit-broken so latency-sensitive callers can degrade up front
LLM endpoint API keys MUST be write-only secrets: accepted on SetAsync, never returned by GetAsync, and resolved at call time rather than stored on LlmEndpoint records
LLM endpoints SHOULD support SHA-256 certificate pinning via CertThumbprint and short connect timeouts so self-signed home endpoints fail fast and pin exactly
Per-endpoint circuit breakers MUST open after consecutive failures for a cooldown window so a dead endpoint does not cost a connect-timeout on every call
MCP LLM invoke tools (llm_embed, llm_rerank, llm_chat) MUST require llm:invoke; registry tools (llm_config_get/upsert) MUST require llm:admin; adapters MUST depend only on LlmRouter.Contract
Non-agent consumers MUST be able to call the router via OpenAI-compatible POST /v1/chat/completions authenticated with llm:invoke; streaming MUST NOT be supported yet
The OpenAI chat completions path MUST forbid cross-project (*) keys because project is taken only from the key claim and is not present in the URL
LLM router DI MUST register unconditionally; Feature.LlmRouter MUST gate the MCP/REST surface, not service registration
Chat routes SHOULD declare optional Thinking (enabled|disabled|provider-default) so reasoning mode is explicit rather than depending on provider model defaults
Deploy control-plane MUST expose a single IDeployService entry point for agent endpoints, MCP tools, and Razor pages; Web MUST NOT reach DeployDb directly
Deploy storage MUST be a single fleet-wide mutable database (not per-project scoped) because one node hosts containers from many projects
quote:
// Deploy: single FLEET-WIDE mutable db (one node hosts containers from many
		// projects, so NOT per-project scoped). Schema ensured once at startup in Configure().

claim: Node agents MUST use a pull-only contract: GET /agent/poll for desired state and POST /agent/heartbeat for actuals, authorized by agent:poll / agent:heartbeat on a node-scoped key
Node-minted agent keys MUST carry agent:poll, agent:heartbeat, and logs:ingest scopes and MUST NOT require config:read because env is resolved server-side at poll
quote:
// Scopes a freshly-minted node key carries: poll desired state, report heartbeat, ship
	// container logs. NO config:read — env is resolved server-side at poll (see PollAsync).

claim: On poll, the control plane MUST resolve each deployment's container env server-side from (Project, ConfigTags) so the node key needs no config:read and avoids project-claim mismatch
quote:
// Resolve each deployment's env server-side (config-resolve over (Project, ConfigTags))
		// so the node key needs no config:read and there is no project-claim mismatch.

claim: A poll response MUST be treated as the full desired set for the node: the agent MUST stop any container whose Service is not in the list (self-fencing)
A heartbeat MUST be a full snapshot of managed containers; services absent from the report MUST be marked Missing so stale Running/healthy state cannot persist

Failover MUST relocate only relocatable deployments from stale nodes onto online nodes covering RequiredTags, preserving one-copy-per-node; failure mode MUST be double-run via self-fence, never data loss
DeployFailoverSweeper MUST run only when Feature.Deploy is enabled, with ~90s staleness (~3 missed polls) and a 30s tick
RunSpec MUST be a structurally allowlisted compose-subset (ports/volumes/restart/healthcheck/resources/network/command/labels/site) with no privileged/cap-add/extra-args escape hatch
Deployments MUST enforce one copy per (Service, NodeId); ConfigHash MUST be computed server-side from image, config tags, desired state, project, and canonical RunSpec JSON
quote:
// Create (Id null/empty) or update (Id set) a deployment. Enforces one deployment per
	// (Service, NodeId). Computes ConfigHash. Returns the resulting view.

claim: HostReport fields from agents MUST be report-only (security/resources/os); the agent MUST NOT auto-fix sshd or other host posture that risks lockout
Deploy MCP tools MUST be fleet-wide (no per-project claim), require deploy:read for reads and deploy:write for writes, and MUST NOT be used by node-agents (agents use /agent/*)
Feature-gated modules MUST register endpoints and BackgroundServices only when Features:<Name> is enabled so a disabled subsystem incurs zero runtime cost for those surfaces
Program-level endpoint mapping MUST gate Config, Logging, Tasks/Sessions, Memory, LlmRouter, Dashboard, Data, and Deploy behind FeatureFlags so disabled modules expose no public surface

Cross-cutting data hygiene services (orphan cleanup for tasks/memory/sessions, BackupService) SHOULD remain unconditional even when features are later disabled, because files can outlive a feature flag
Portable agent-definition REST surfaces MUST stay always-on (Core DB) without a feature flag
quote:
// Portable agent-definition store (Core DB; always on — no feature flag).
		PetBox.Web.AgentDefs.AgentDefsApi.MapAgentDefsEndpoints(app);

claim: .NET client SDKs MUST send the canonical X-Api-Key header (server may still accept legacy X-YobaConf-ApiKey / Bearer/Token)
PetBox.Client MUST provide a typed Data client for raw parameterized SQL pass-through plus DataDb provisioning; Config MUST live in the specialized PetBox.Client.Config package on the same transport
Data-module clients (.NET/TS/Python) MUST treat the server as a dumb SQL pass-through that knows nothing about caller POCOs/types and only runs parameterized SQL against the project's SQLite file
TS and Python Data clients MUST mirror the .NET PetBoxDataClient surface (query/exec/createDb/schema) for cross-language parity
quote:
/**
 * TypeScript client for the PetBox Data module — raw parameterized SQL over HTTP plus DataDb
 * provisioning. Mirrors the .NET PetBoxDataClient. The server is a dumb pass-through: it knows
 * nothing about your types, it just runs the SQL against the project's SQLite file.

claim: Config clients MUST poll /v1/conf with ETag/If-None-Match, support tag-vector resolve (not a single hierarchical path), and support the four response templates (flat, dotnet, envvar, envvar-deep)
quote:
/**
 * TypeScript SDK client for PetBox config.
 *
 * Fetches resolved config from /v1/conf with ETag-aware polling. Supports all four
 * response templates (flat, dotnet, envvar, envvar-deep).

claim: Config client LKG disk cache MUST let a host boot on last-known-good config after restart even while PetBox is unreachable (DC-outage survival), with best-effort cache I/O that never breaks loading
Config client initial fetch SHOULD use a short timeout (default 10s) so an unreachable PetBox never blocks host startup for long; Optional=true SHOULD allow empty-start + retry instead of fail-fast
Python petbox-client MUST ship with zero runtime dependencies (stdlib only), mirroring the TypeScript SDK's no-runtime-deps stance for config/data clients
quote:
# Zero runtime dependencies — stdlib only (urllib + threading), mirroring the TS SDK.
dependencies = []

claim: Published client package versions in-repo MAY be placeholder 0.0.0 for npm/PyPI packages and rely on the release pipeline (GitVersion / publish targets) rather than hand-maintained package versions
quote:
"name": "@stdray-npm/petbox-client",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "TypeScript SDK for PetBox — config client (ETag-aware polling) and a Data-module client (raw parameterized SQL pass-through).",

claim: TS Data client MUST send X-Api-Key while the TS config client currently still documents/sends legacy X-YobaConf-ApiKey — a dual-header reality clients must tolerate until fully normalized
quote: /** Plaintext API key. Sent as the X-Api-Key header on every request. */

claim: Deploy control-plane HTTP surface MUST map only /agent/poll, /agent/heartbeat, and /api/deploy/nodes at the REST layer; richer operator CRUD is exposed via MCP/UI on IDeployService
LLM registry MUST be config-stored (endpoints + routes as JSON), not hardcoded; MCP llm_config_upsert replaces the project's registry after validation
quote:
// The full router registry: the endpoints and the routes that order them per capability.
// Persisted as JSON in the Config module (llm-config-driven) — configurable, not hardcoded.

claim: MCP tool registration MUST discover tools via reflection from the Web assembly (Mcp/*Tools.cs) with schema-honest options rather than the stock WithToolsFromAssembly seam
FeatureFlags.IsEnabled MUST treat a feature as disabled unless Features:<Name> is explicitly true in configuration
quote:
public bool IsEnabled(Feature feature) =>
		_flags.TryGetValue(feature.ToString(), out var enabled) && enabled;

claim: Operator deploy_node_upsert with mintKey=true MUST mint or rotate a node-scoped agent key and return the plaintext key once
quote: [Description("Registers or updates a node. With mintKey=true also mints (or rotates) the node-scoped agent key and returns it ONCE. Requires deploy:write.")]

claim: Legacy agent heartbeats with null Capabilities MUST NOT erase capabilities previously reported by a capability-aware agent
quote:
// Capabilities are agent-detected host facts; only a capability-aware agent (non-null)
		// overwrites them, so a legacy agent's heartbeat doesn't erase what a newer one found.

claim: SiteSpec on RunSpec MUST mark a deployment as a site behind the host reverse proxy (domain + port), with the agent keeping Caddy config in line
MCP JSON tool results SHOULD use a relaxed encoder so non-ASCII (e.g. Cyrillic) stays human-readable rather than \uXXXX-escaped, while HTML-sensitive chars remain escaped
report_issue MUST require Feature.Tasks enabled because reports are filed through ITasksService into the tasks boards
quote:
ModuleMcp.AssertFeature(features, Feature.Tasks);
		if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(title)) throw new ArgumentException("title is required");

claim: Python Data client MUST use X-Api-Key while the Python config client uses legacy X-YobaConf-ApiKey, matching the dual-header transitional state also present in TS
quote: _API_KEY_HEADER = "X-Api-Key"

claim: Default config refresh interval SHOULD be five minutes with ETag 304s keeping polls cheap; RefreshInterval=0 SHOULD disable polling (one-shot load)
ILlmRegistryAdmin and CapabilityRouter registration MUST keep breakers and cert-pinned HttpClients as singletons so health state and handlers outlive a single request scope
A node is online if LastSeenAt is within a 120s window; poll bumps LastSeenAt and is the liveness signal failover watches
LLM MCP tools/list classification currently leaves llm_* unclassified (fail-open show-all); call-time still enforces llm:invoke/llm:admin — list economy does not yet module-trim LLM tools
quote:
static string? ModuleOf(string tool) =>
		tool.StartsWith("tasks_", StringComparison.Ordinal) || tool.StartsWith("session_", StringComparison.Ordinal) ? "tasks"
		: tool.StartsWith("memory_", StringComparison.Ordinal) ? "memory"
		: tool.StartsWith("log_", StringComparison.Ordinal) ? "logs"
		: tool.StartsWith("data_", StringComparison.Ordinal) || tool.StartsWith("db_", StringComparison.Ordinal) ? "data"
		: tool.StartsWith("deploy_", StringComparison.Ordinal) ? "deploy"
		: tool.StartsWith("agent_def_", StringComparison.Ordinal) ? "agents"
		: tool.StartsWith("config_", StringComparison.Ordinal) ? ApiKeyScopes.AdminProvision
		: null; // project_* / apikey_* — provisioning-mixed (admin:provision shows ALL anyway), leave shown

claim: Log event records MUST be append-only: new events are bulk-inserted; the store has no update path for existing LogEntries and ages rows out via retention delete only.
quote: the log store is append-only with retention, so unnormalized rows age out and the store self-heals.

claim: A named log MUST be created before ingest; path-based CLEF/OTLP/Seq-compat MUST NOT auto-vivify missing logs (404 'create it first').
quote:
if (!await store.ExistsAsync(projectKey, logName, ct))
			return Results.NotFound(new ErrorResponse($"log '{logName}' not found in project '{projectKey}'; create it first"));

claim: Only the $system self-log MAY be auto-created at boot; all other named logs MUST be created explicitly (API/MCP/admin).
quote:
// The petbox self-log is the one log created automatically — petbox's
			// own ILogger + Seq self-log write here. User logs are created explicitly.

claim: A named log MUST be addressed as (projectKey, logName) with on-disk file at logs/{projectKey}/{logName}.db.
quote:
// Well-known log identifiers. A log is addressed by (projectKey, logName); the
// file lives at logs/{projectKey}/{logName}.db.

claim: Log names MUST match ^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,99}$ (same name spec as DataDbs).
Creating a named log MUST materialize the SQLite file + schema eagerly (no implicit create-on-first-write).
quote:
// Materialize the file + schema eagerly (no implicit create-on-first-write).
		_factory.NewEnsuredConnection(projectKey, logName).Dispose();

claim: The reserved self-log MUST be named 'petbox' in project '$system' and REST MUST refuse deleting it.
quote:
if (projectKey == LogNames.SystemProject && name == LogNames.SelfLog)
			return Results.BadRequest(new ErrorResponse("the petbox self-log cannot be deleted"));

claim: Path-based CLEF ingest MUST require X-Service-Key (free emitter string; no Service entity) and logs:ingest + project scope.
Bare OTLP /v1/{logs,traces,metrics} paths MUST serve PetBox self-export only: X-Seq-ApiKey validated against Seq:SelfLog:ApiKey, routing into the $system self-log.
OTLP path-based ingest MUST authorize the caller's project claim against the route projectKey (any-key cross-project inject forbidden).
Properties/Attributes bags MUST normalize keys at BOTH write and KQL search boundaries so a stored key and a requested key always meet in the same form.
Flat dotted property keys (e.g. http.route, petbox.request_chars) MUST resolve natively: dots are kept; only quotes/backslashes/control chars are replaced with '_'.
When distinct original property keys collide after normalization, write MUST assign deterministic '_2', '_3', … suffixes in encounter order (first original keeps the bare normalized name).
KQL joins/correlation subqueries MUST stay within the same log table root; cross-log/table joins MUST NOT be supported.
Events-shaped REST/MCP results MUST pin PascalCase field names (Id, ServiceKey, Timestamp, Level, …) to match KQL schema identifiers used by table-shaped column names.
KQL parse failures MUST map to 400 with per-diagnostic details; unsupported constructs to 400; engine/execution faults MUST map to structured JSON 500 (never HTML error page).
MCP log_query MUST be a thin adapter over ILogQueryService (shared with REST) and MUST NOT open LogDb directly; catalog lifecycle lives in separate log_create/list/delete tools.
Per-project log retention MUST cascade (project → workspace → system); default RetentionDays=7, $system uses SystemRetainDays=30, sweeper interval system-only.
RetentionService MUST delete LogEntries older than the cutoff per named log, expired ShareLinks, and HealthReports older than dashboard.healthRetentionDays.
Orphan log .db files (and .wal/.shm) with no Logs metadata row MUST be periodically removed (mirrors Data orphan cleanup).
Ingestion MUST use a per-named-log bounded channel pipeline (capacity/batch from IngestionSettings; writers block when full; BulkCopyAsync flush).
Live-tail MUST stream newly ingested events as SSE for a named log; subscriber buffer MUST DropOldest with default WindowSize=200.
Share links MUST be opaque tokens; GET /api/share/{token}/tsv MUST be anonymous and return NotFound for missing/expired links.
quote: app.MapGet("/api/share/{token}/tsv", GetTsvAsync).AllowAnonymous();

claim: Share TSV export MUST apply per-column MaskMode (Keep/Mask/Hide) with HMAC-salted Mask values; Hide columns MUST be omitted.
Share export MUST still hard-cap materialization at KqlLimits.MaxTake (no default take; exports stored KQL but never unbounded).
Creating a share MUST authorize ProjectKey from the body against the caller's project claim (any-authenticated cross-project mint forbidden).
Health reports MUST be append-only, identified by (Svc, canonical Tags); both push (POST /api/health) and pull (HealthPoller) APPEND HealthReports.
Health tag identity MUST use a canonical comma-separated key:value string sorted by key (same flavour as config tags).
quote:
// Canonical form for health-report tags: comma-separated "key:value" sorted by
// key (same flavour as config tags). Identity of a running thing = (Svc, canonical tags).

claim: Health push MUST require tags.project and health:write; a project-scoped key may only report for its own project.
HealthPoller MUST periodically GET each enabled HealthEndpoint URL, expect status structure { svc, name?, tags{}, version?, sha?, buildDate?, status }, and append HealthReport with Source="pull".
On pull, the HealthEndpoint project ownership MUST force tags["project"] = endpoint.ProjectKey (payload project tag cannot override).
On fetch/parse failure HealthPoller MUST skip writing a report (no synthetic down row); staleness is inferred from aging latest report.
quote:
// PetBox.Web.Health.HealthApi (POST /api/health). On fetch/parse failure we skip
// — the status page flags a (svc,tags) whose latest report has gone stale.

claim: Health poll interval MUST be system-level DashboardSettings with minimum 5 seconds; request timeout and stale threshold are also system-only.
Health report history retention MUST be configurable via dashboard.healthRetentionDays (default 30) and swept by RetentionService.
MCP health_search MUST expose latest report per (svc, canonical tags) with age/stale flags; optional window/limit turns on per-service history (default history off).
quote:
[McpServerTool(Name = "health_search", Title = "Read service health reports", ReadOnly = true, UseStructuredContent = true, OutputSchemaType = typeof(HealthSearchResultView))]
	[Description("Reads the latest health report per running service in a project (status, version, age, stale flag), with time in ISO-8601 UTC. Identity of a service is (svc, canonical tags). Optionally returns per-service history when `window` (seconds) and/or `limit` are supplied. Requires health:read scope; a project-scoped key sees only its own project, a cross-project ('*') key any project.")]

claim: health_search timestamps MUST be normalized to ISO-8601 UTC on read only (SQLite stores space-separated text; staleness computed in memory, never SQL string compare against 'T' form).

Dashboard UI latest-service view MUST group by (Svc, project tag) treating remaining tags as volatile payload of a single report (not identity), unlike health_search which groups by full canonical Tags.
HealthEndpoints are pull-mode only configuration; push-mode sources need no endpoint row and POST /api/health directly.
When Features:Logging is disabled, log ingestion pipeline, retention, and orphan cleanup MUST NOT be registered (zero runtime cost).
When Features:Dashboard is disabled, HealthPoller MUST NOT be registered.
CiPoller is referenced as a future Dashboard phase registration but MUST NOT be claimed as shipped — only HealthPoller is registered under Feature.Dashboard.
quote: // Phase 4: register HealthPoller, CiPoller

claim: Partial CLEF batch success MUST still ingest valid candidates; only all-lines-failed yields 400 IngestRejectedResponse.
REST live-tail MUST require logs:query + project authorization and emit text/event-stream rows with data-event-id for newly written records.

Query path for events/spans/metrics is ALWAYS scoped to one named log DB — there is no cross-log addressing operator in LogQueryService (projectKey+logName select the store, roots select tables inside it).
PropertiesJsonSerializer is the single WRITE boundary for event property bags (CLEF/seq/raw-envelope/self-log): keys normalized once before storage via NameAllocator.
Share pages / Apply path are events-only: unsupported table message claims only 'events' (unlike LogQueryService which lists events/spans/metrics).
Path-based CLEF ingest MUST enforce project claim + logs:ingest (ApiKey policy alone is insufficient to authorize destination project).
Dashboard page MUST load only metadata counts for logs/dbs/keys (no log/data file opens) plus latest health per project for the workspace grid.
quote:
// Cheap per-project counts (petbox.db metadata only — no log/data file opens).
	public IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int> LogCount { get; private set; } = new Dictionary<string, int>();

claim: OTLP logs path-based ingest MUST require X-Service-Key; traces/metrics path-based ingest do not require it (parsed from protobuf resource/attributes).
ILogQueryService is the single shared KQL orchestration for REST and MCP; adapters keep only auth, wire shape, and error mapping.
Note: RetentionService deletes only LogEntries older than cutoff — Spans/MetricPoints retention is not swept by the same loop (events-only retention as shipped).
Note: MCP log_delete does not special-case the self-log undeletable guard present on REST DeleteLogAsync — residual surface gap if agents call log_delete on $system/petbox.
OTLP path routes RequireAuthorization("ApiKey") but (as shipped) do not re-check logs:ingest scope inside handlers — only project claim is Asserted; CLEF path checks LogsIngest explicitly.
quote:
static async Task<IResult> IngestLogs(
		HttpContext ctx,
		string projectKey,
		string logName,
		ILogStore store,
		IIngestionPipeline pipeline,
		CancellationToken ct)
	{
		if (!AuthorizeProject(ctx, projectKey, out var forbid)) return forbid;

claim: Memory entry Type MUST be one of the closed taxonomy User|Feedback|Project|Reference (mutually exclusive; distinct from free-form Tags).
quote: Memory entry taxonomy — mirrors the coding-agent memory schema (user | feedback | project | reference). A closed, mutually-exclusive classification (drives recall filtering and write-time guidance), distinct from the open free-form Tags.

claim: Memory writes MUST reject an invalid Type string with an explicit allowed-set error (User|Feedback|Project|Reference).
quote:
static MemoryType ParseType(string s) =>
		Enum.TryParse<MemoryType>(s, ignoreCase: true, out var v)
			? v
			: throw new ArgumentException($"invalid type '{s}' (User|Feedback|Project|Reference)");

claim: A new memory entry (version 0) MUST require Type; an edit MAY omit Type and keep the active entry's type.
quote: Type = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(i.Type) ? ParseType(i.Type) : current?.Type ?? throw new ArgumentException("type is required for new entries"),

claim: Memory Tags on the wire MUST be arrays; null = keep (PATCH), [] = clear, non-empty list REPLACES; service MUST normalize (trim/lowercase/dedup) at the boundary.
quote: Tags is an ARRAY of tag strings (the memory surface speaks arrays, like tasks): null = omit (PATCH: keep the current set), [] = explicit clear, a non-empty list REPLACES the set. The service normalizes (trim/lowercase/dedup) and joins to the CSV storage form at the boundary

claim: Memory Metadata MUST be opaque free-form JSON string and MUST NOT be FTS-indexed.
quote:
// Free-form structured metadata (JSON string) for round-tripping arbitrary
	// client key/values; opaque to the service and NOT FTS-indexed.

claim: Memory entry edits MUST use PATCH semantics: omitted (null) fields keep current values; explicit empty clears; new entries map null to empty.
Memory soft-delete MUST close the active temporal revision (history kept); Version 0 deletes regardless of baseline.
quote:
// A soft-delete request: close the active entry at Key (Version 0 = regardless).
public sealed record MemoryDelete(string Key, long Version);

claim: Memory upsert write-ack MUST echo ONLY this call's Added/Updated/Removed keys (no write-side cursor/delta); CurrentVersion remains the store-wide cursor for DeltaAsync.
When a memory write does not apply, the ack MUST empty Added/Updated/Removed so Applied=false is unambiguous; rebase via memory_delta.
Memory Class-A lexical index MUST update inside the entity transaction; Class-B vectors MUST materialize off the write path so a down embedder never blocks or loses a write.
The agent memory canon MUST live in store "canon" as a compact pointer index; UpsertAsync MUST reject bodies over a hard 10000-char budget covering both memory_upsert and memory_remember.
Canon write-gate MUST only measure supplied bodies on PATCH (omitted body keeps current in-budget value) and MUST NOT apply to non-canon stores.
GET /api/memory/{projectKey}/canon MUST return both project and workspace canon parts (store canon, key index); missing parts MUST be null with HTTP 200.
Shared cross-project memory MUST address the reserved "$workspace" container; MCP scope workspace MUST resolve there (distinct from "$system" so project cascade does not inherit all $system memory).
memory_search with no scope MUST CASCADE project ⊕ workspace (rows labelled by scope, project first); explicit project|workspace narrows to one container.
Key-addressed $workspace memory curation MUST be limited to keys whose project claim is "*" or a project in the same workspace as the $workspace row.
memory_remember MUST always CREATE a new entry (generated m- GUID key); edits MUST go via memory_upsert. Default store is notes; default type Project.
quote:
CREATE one durable fact verbatim (always a NEW entry; edits go via memory_upsert).
		`scope`: project (default) | workspace (cross-project shared). `type` taxonomy
		(User|Feedback|Project|Reference) — pick explicitly. Store durable facts not derivable
		from code/git/config; actionable work goes to a task board. Requires memory:write.

claim: Implicit multi-store memory sweep MUST exclude sensitive stores ops and session-digests; explicit Filter.Store MUST still reach any store; canon and autocaptured MUST remain in default recall.
System memory stores session-digests, autocaptured, and canon MUST be tagged IsSystem; IsSystem MUST gate badge/delete-guard only, not entry writes or the implicit search sweep.
Memory store names MUST match ^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,99}$; catalog create is explicit, but the upsert path MAY auto-vivify a missing store via EnsureAsync.
IMemoryUsageRecorder MUST be called only by agent/human-facing adapters; internal machine consumers MUST reach IMemoryService directly so counters stay honest.
Deliberate search impressions MUST increment DeliberateCount; machine/hook pulls (usage:machine) MUST bump only SurfacedCount. Default is deliberate.
Usage recorder MUST enqueue asynchronously (bounded channel; drop on overflow) so telemetry never slows or takes down the read path.
Memory usage aggregate MUST report coverage, deliberate coverage, median last-hit timestamp, and oldest-first dead-tail sample over the ACTIVE entry set only.
Quarantine GC MUST only touch the autocaptured store; candidates MUST be older than MinAge with zero Deliberate and zero Opened (machine Surfaced alone does not protect).
Quarantine GC default MUST be report-only (Enforce=false); enforce mode MUST soft-delete via IMemoryService so history is kept and recovery is possible.
SessionFactsJob autocapture MUST quarantine machine writes to store autocaptured only; curated stores (e.g. notes) MUST never be updated or deleted by the machine judge.
Autocapture MUST extract only durable facts not derivable from code/git/boards and MUST drop narration/progress/session bookkeeping.
quote:
DURABILITY TEST — before emitting anything, ask: "would a FUTURE agent, a month from
		now, need this — and is it NOT already derivable from the code, git history, or the
		task/spec boards?" If it is derivable there, it is NOT a fact. Keep distinctive
		identifiers/slugs/error texts VERBATIM.

		Do NOT extract (these are the common false positives):
		  • narration of work done — "реализовано/задеплоено/добавлен пакет X/wave N готова",
		    "смок прошёл", ci numbers, commit hashes, "переименовал файл", executed plans;
		  • progress/status reports and per-session bookkeeping (what was tried this session,
		    which tests ran, transient TODOs);
		  • anything a `git log`, the code, or a board query would already answer.
		A record of THIS session's activity is not durable knowledge — the session transcript
		already holds it.

claim: Session storage MUST be latest-snapshot last-write-wins (no SCD-2); Version MUST equal last message ordinal as a monotonic content cursor, not optimistic concurrency.
Session AppendAsync MUST treat fromOrdinal contiguity: equal last+1 appends; overlap is idempotent (existing wins); gap MUST write nothing with Applied=false and LastOrdinal for resend.
Session soft-delete MUST keep the row (DeletedAt audit) but hide it from every read; a re-push of the same SessionId MUST resurrect it.
Session MetaJson MUST accept only a JSON object (observed client metadata); non-null non-empty last-write-wins; null/whitespace MUST keep existing MetaJson.
SessionId ResolveIdAsync MUST prefer exact match over prefix; ambiguous prefixes MUST return Ambiguous ids (not guessed); writes MUST address exact ids only.
Session discovery digests MUST land in memory store session-digests; the digest entry metadata MUST act as the distillation cursor over message ordinals via DeltaAsync.
Digest text MUST keep distinctive terms verbatim for FTS and format as one title line plus up to 30 "- " fact lines in the session's language.
quote:
You maintain the discovery digest of one AI-agent work session. You are given the
		current digest (possibly empty) and new transcript messages; return the complete
		updated digest, merging new facts into the existing ones (update, don't duplicate).
		Format: the FIRST line is one short sentence naming what the session is about, in
		the session's own language. Then up to 30 lines, each "- " + one concrete fact:
		goals, decisions and their why, outcomes, errors and fixes, and the entities
		touched (files, modules, branches, commands, tools, URLs, ids). Keep distinctive
		terms VERBATIM (identifiers, slugs, error texts) — the digest is matched by
		full-text search.

claim: Session stage-1 discovery MUST fuse digest + verbatim term (+ optional fullscan) rankings with the same RRF primitive; term leg MUST run even when no digest store exists.
ISessionTermIndex MUST index raw transcript content independently of LLM digests and MUST backfill on Version cursor without requiring chat capability.
Session full-scan MUST require per-call fullScan:true AND SessionFullScanSettings SystemEnabled AND ProjectEnabled (both default off; independent non-cascading switches).
When fullScan is requested but not allowed, session search MUST report fullScanRequested=true, fullScanRan=false, fullScanReason="not-allowed" (never silently ignore).
Full-scan MUST cap scanned sessions (default MaxSessions=200, most-recently-updated first) and MUST surface Capped when the project holds more sessions than the cap.
Episodic session search MUST use a transient per-session index hydrated on demand and aged out by idleness (no always-on global transcript index).
Episodic hits MUST carry message ordinal as the provenance bridge to session_get for the verbatim source.
Episodic semantic leg MUST exclude messages shorter than MinSemanticChars from embedding/candidates while lexical still indexes short messages; SemanticFloor MUST floor only semantic-only hits.
DuckDbSessionEpisodicIndex MUST fuse in-memory Russian-stem BM25 FTS with brute-force MRL-1024 cosine vectors; message embeddings MUST cache by (sessionId, ordinal) content hash across hydrations.
Core SearchService Synchronous indexes MUST update inside the entity transaction; Eventual indexes MUST NOT be driven on the write path and search MUST degrade honestly when a leg fails.
HybridMerge RRF MUST score as Σ 1/(K+rank0) with K=60 so fused scores are comparable across independent per-container pools for global merge.
Memory query mode MUST apply semantic-noise floor on raw RRF score before freshness decay, then freshness-blend and MMR-diversify; lexically confirmed hits MUST never be floored.
Shared search re-ranking MUST support Recency half-life (default 30d enabled), MMR diversity (lambda 0.7 enabled; silent identity without vectors), and Floor SemanticFloor (default 0.0155).
memory_search with q MUST honestly merge project ⊕ workspace pools by fused score (each scope gets full cap); listing MUST prefer project-first remainder cascade.
session_search listing requires tasks:read; query mode MUST also require Feature.Memory and memory:read (two-stage pipeline leans on Memory).
Memory search Type filter MUST apply post-resolution because index Type is a constant "memory" (MemoryType is not pushed into the search index).
A search/listing WITHOUT a query MUST reject sort-by-relevance; default listing order MUST be Updated desc because memory keys are opaque generated ids.
quote:
if (query is null && request.Sort is { By: MemorySortBy.Relevance })
			throw new ArgumentException("sort by relevance needs a query (q) — without one the read is a deterministic listing (default order: updated desc)");

claim: Per-store hybrid candidate pool for multi-store memory query MUST be max(3×limit, 50) before global RRF fusion across stores.
Memory orphan cleanup MUST reclaim deleted projects' memory-store .db files and empty project directories left after ProjectDeletion bulk metadata delete.
Session messages MUST be server-numbered ordinals 1..N; client SessionMessageInput carries only Role+Content.
Term/fullscan-only discovery candidates MUST count as LexicalConfirmed so they are never floored as semantic-only noise.
session_search Distilled=false MUST mean no digest store yet (not "no matches"); candidates MUST still be returned from term/fullscan legs.
Quarantine GC scan interval default MUST be 6 hours and MinAge default 30 days.
quote:
public static readonly TimeSpan DefaultMinAge = TimeSpan.FromDays(30);
	public static readonly TimeSpan DefaultScanInterval = TimeSpan.FromHours(6);

claim: memory_get of a missing key MUST raise a not-found error (never bare null) because declared outputSchema demands structured content.
quote:
Get the active entry by key. A missing/unknown key is a not-found ERROR (never a bare
		null: a declared outputSchema demands structured content, so a null result is rejected
		by strict MCP clients — the error rides the isError channel instead).

claim: Personal facts MUST use type=User rather than a separate personal container; workspace scope is for cross-project shared facts.
Terminal nodes MUST be hidden under active-only reads unless includeClosed; terminal part_of ancestors of visible nodes MUST be kept so the tree stays connected
quote:
// Hide terminal (closed) nodes unless includeClosed; keep terminal part_of ancestors of
	// any visible node so the tree stays connected. `underId` restricts to a part_of subtree.

claim: Missing transition errors MUST list NextFrom of the current status
quote: return WorkflowResult.Fail($"no transition '{fromSlug}' -> '{toSlug}'; from '{fromSlug}' you can go to: {string.Join("|", wf.NextFrom(fromSlug!))}");

claim: Node listing order MUST be sparse Priority then Key by default (lower priority first)
quote:
// A node in a board's tree, stored as a temporal (SCD type-2) row. Identity (Key)
// is the path "a/b/c"; ordering is sparse Priority then Key.

claim: Spec delivery MUST combine a node's own linked tasks with each child's recursively-computed delivery over the part_of tree; a leaf with no linked tasks MUST be not_started
quote: Bottom-up, memoized: a node's delivery combines its OWN directly-linked tasks (if any) with each child's recursively-computed delivery, via the same CombineDelivery used for tag-group rollups. A leaf with no linked tasks has no inputs at all, so CombineDelivery defaults it to not_started — it no longer disappears into a flat subtree-wide task union (which used to hide task-less leaves and let an umbrella wrongly read as `done` when some leaves had no work whatsoever).

claim: Kinds with MethodologyDeliveryDef MUST compute bottom-up delivery from inbound task_spec links: not_started / in_progress / done / done_with_defects
quote: Delivery roll-up as DATA (spec primitives-enum-residual): how linked task_spec nodes contribute to a board's computed delivery. `RequiredTypes` drive progress (none present → not_started; any non-TerminalOk → in_progress; all TerminalOk → candidate for done); any `DefectTypes` still Open while requireds are done yield done_with_defects.

claim: tasks_upsert MUST accept priority as a sparse int with lower-first ordering; anti-starvation is NOT productized
quote: priority? (sparse int, lower first), version (WATERMARK baseline: pass the

claim: blocks transition effects MUST consume the edge and release only the last blocker
quote: // gating semantics: consume the edge; release only the last blocker

claim: Deleting a node MUST soft-close every edge touching it and its tags, and if it was a blocker MUST move targets left with no blockers from Blocked to InProgress as a system action
Relations list MUST support direction from|to|both and optional includeHistory for soft-closed edges
quote:
// direction: "from" (edges out of nodeId), "to" (edges into nodeId), "both" (default).
	// includeHistory=false returns only active edges (ClosedAt is null).

claim: Part_of parent resolution MUST accept a slug on this board or a known NodeId (cross-board allowed within the project file)
quote:
// Resolve a PartOf / Supersedes value to a NodeId: a slug on this board, else an existing
	// NodeId (cross-board allowed — the project file holds every board's nodes).

claim: An accepted RequiresReason reason MUST be persisted as an artifact:reason system comment on the node
Chore on work boards MUST mean spec-less engineering hygiene in the MCP contract
quote: type (feature|bug|chore on work boards; chore = spec-less engineering hygiene),

claim: MCP tasks_upsert MUST document blockedBy, supersedes, commits, priority, deleted:true, and flat key+partOf nesting as the write contract
quote: blockedBy (the blocking node as its slug on THIS board or a NodeId — the same slug-or-NodeId convention as specRef/partOf), supersedes (a slug|NodeId this node replaces — the old one is moved to its terminal-cancel), commits? (an ARRAY of commit SHAs — hex, 7..40 chars; null omits, [] clears, a list REPLACES the node's full commit set, same PATCH semantics as tags), priority? (sparse int, lower first),

claim: supersedes MUST create a supersedes edge and move the superseded node to its kind's terminal-cancel status as a system effect without the approve gate
Relation create MUST be idempotent for an identical active edge and MUST soft-close on delete (history kept via ClosedAt)
First node write MAY auto-vivify a missing board as a deliberate agent-ergonomics exception to explicit create
quote: Create the board if it does not yet exist; no-op if it does. Used by the upsert write path to auto-vivify on first write (deliberate exception to the explicit-create rule, decided 2026-05-31 for agent ergonomics).

claim: tasks_board_close MUST archive a board so agents stop writing by inertia; history is kept; reopen via tasks_board_reopen
quote: Close a board: it rejects further writes (so agents stop writing to it by inertia) but stays readable; history is kept. Reopen with tasks_board_reopen. Requires tasks:write.

claim: Intake status vocabulary MUST include reported/triage/confirmed/duplicate/wontfix/done with reason gates on terminal cancel paths
quote:
static readonly MethodologyKindDef IntakeKind = new("intake", QuickAddAllowed: true,
	[
		new MethodologyWorkflowDef(["issue"],
			[
				new("reported", "Reported", StatusKind.Open),
				new("triage", "Triage", StatusKind.Open),
				new("confirmed", "Confirmed", StatusKind.Open),
				new("duplicate", "Duplicate", StatusKind.TerminalCancel),
				new("wontfix", "Won't fix", StatusKind.TerminalCancel),
				new("done", "Done", StatusKind.TerminalOk),
			],

claim: MCP relations_create/list/delete MUST exist alongside tasks_* for edge CRUD with instance-scoped kind vocabulary
quote: [McpServerTool(Name = "relations_create", Title = "Create a relation", UseStructuredContent = true, OutputSchemaType = typeof(RelationCreatedResult))]

claim: MCP tasks_board_create MUST accept simple|classic|spec|ideas|intake|work plus instance-declared custom kinds, default simple
quote: CREATE one named task board in a project for a single `kind` (simple|classic|spec|ideas|intake|work, default simple — plus any kind a methodology instance's rules declare).

claim: Board create default kind MUST be simple when kind is omitted
quote: var kindSlug = (kind ?? "simple").Trim().ToLowerInvariant();

claim: Process-role singleton violation MUST direct users to close, adopt elsewhere, or use a simple board
quote:
throw new ArgumentException(
				$"{scope} already has an active {kind.ToString().ToLowerInvariant()} board ('{existing.Name}') — process-role kinds are one-per-instance; close it (tasks_board_close), adopt it elsewhere, or use a simple board");

claim: Unknown board kind at create MUST list runtime.KnownKinds and direct custom kinds to instance rules upsert/create
Work explicit FSM MUST include Pending/InProgress/Review/Done/Blocked/Deferred/Cancelled with Review→Done RequiresApproval and Review↔InProgress reject-back
quote:
new("Pending", "InProgress"),
				new("InProgress", "Review"),
				new("Review", "InProgress"),                       // reject back
				new("Review", "Done", RequiresApproval: true),     // approve gate

claim: A plan node's NodeId MUST be immutable after creation, and its type MUST be immutable once set
Comment delete MUST soft-close the comment and MUST be rejected while active replies remain (leaf-only delete)
quote:
// Soft-close a comment (and its tags). REJECTED if it still has active replies — the
	// caller must delete the children first. Returns false if it was already gone.

claim: Birth into an approval-gated TerminalOk status under global enforceApproval, or into the target of an ENFORCED approval transition, MUST require a maintainer-capable actor
Simple type vocabulary MUST be enforced at write door with default task; invalid type is rejected listing valid SimpleTypes
Delete of a node with active part_of children MUST return applied:false with a Rejected conflict (not an exception) unless children are deleted in the same batch
Spec-node soft-delete MUST NOT require ideaRef; erasing junk is not a spec change (retiring a real requirement stays deprecated)
quote: Spec-node deletes need no ideaRef (erasing junk is not a spec change — retiring a real requirement stays `deprecated`).

claim: part_of MUST enforce a single active parent, allow detach via empty string, and MUST reject cycles
A comment reply MUST hang under an active parent comment of the same board and node; intra-batch parents are not supported
Ideas review→rejected and exploring→rejected MUST require a reason
quote:
new("review", "rejected", RequiresReason: true),
				new("exploring", "rejected", RequiresReason: true),

claim: A transition with RequiresReason MUST reject the write unless a non-empty reason field is provided on that call (never the node body)
quote: if (tr.RequiresReason && !hasReason) return WorkflowResult.Fail($"transition '{fromSlug}' -> '{toSlug}' requires a reason (provide a non-empty reason field on this call)");

claim: NodePatch.Reason MUST be first-class for RequiresReason transitions and MUST NOT be merged into the node body
Process-role kinds MUST allow at most one open board per process-role kind per methodology instance; classic and simple MUST be unlimited
quote: Process-role singleton: ≤1 open board of a process-role kind per instance (or per legacy null-membership bucket). classic|simple are unlimited.

claim: Process-role singleton MUST apply inside the instance, not project-wide
quote: Process-role singleton (≤1 open board per process-role kind) applies INSIDE the instance, not project-wide.

claim: Process-role singleton MUST still apply when a definition-declared kind maps onto a process-role BoardKind even if the kind is stored verbatim
quote: A definition-declared kind is stored VERBATIM. Process-role singleton still applies when the kind maps onto a process-role BoardKind.

claim: A closed board MUST reject writes until reopened while remaining readable with history
quote: if (meta.ClosedAt != null) throw new InvalidOperationException($"board '{board}' is closed — reopen it (tasks_board_reopen) before writing");

claim: Board names MUST match ^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,99}$
quote:
// Same name spec as logs/data dbs: starts a-z, then a-z/0-9/_/- up to 100 chars.
	[GeneratedRegex(@"^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,99}$")]

claim: Work and Spec boards MUST reject bare quick-add (QuickAddAllowed false) because nodes need a link at birth
quote: Quick-add is rejected: a work node needs a specRef at birth the bare form can't supply.

claim: Intake triage→duplicate and triage→wontfix MUST require a reason; classic Duplicate MUST require a reason
quote:
new("triage", "duplicate", RequiresReason: true),
				new("triage", "wontfix", RequiresReason: true),

claim: A work node entering Blocked MUST name a blocker via blockedBy or already have an active blocks edge
quote: throw new ArgumentException($"a Blocked task must name a blocker — provide blockedBy (node '{n.Key}')");

claim: Work type MUST be required at write time; empty type yields null workflow (type required contract)
quote: Work is STRICT: type selects the workflow and an unknown/empty type yields null (the "type required" contract).

claim: Auto-wire of SpecBoard MUST run only within the same methodology-instance membership bucket so multi-instance projects never cross-wire work→spec
Quartet delivery type roles MUST be requiredTypes=feature and defectTypes=bug (code authority; chore does not drive progress)
NetArch tests MUST forbid Web tools/pages from reaching ITaskBoardStore / IRelationStore / TasksDb directly
quote: A NetArchTest forbids Web tools/pages from reaching ITaskBoardStore / IRelationStore / TasksDb directly, so this interface is the only door.

claim: WorkflowEngine is THE single validation point for unknown status and missing transitions
quote:
// THE single validation point for task status / transitions. Both "unknown status"
// and "no such transition" are decided here so the rule lives in one place.

claim: TasksActor MUST model writer capability (CanApprove) not identity; MCP maps tasks:approve, UI maps cookie-authenticated owner
All boards of a project MUST share one tasks/<project>.db file partitioned by Board column
quote: All of a project's boards share one file (tasks/<project>.db); a board's nodes are the rows whose Board column equals its name.

claim: Legacy free kind strings MUST map to Simple via ParseKind fallback
quote: Simple is first so it stays the ParseKind fallback — a legacy "free" string maps to it.

claim: Agent ceiling of Review (never Done) is dogfood process policy; product server-blocks only when EnforceApproval is set (presets do not)
quote: **An agent never sets the final `Done` itself — its ceiling is `Review`.** The maintainer confirms.

claim: Anti-starvation / iteration backlog selection by priority+blockers is methodology dogfood policy, not a Tasks engine capability
quote: An iteration = a **filtered backlog** (pull a batch by: no blockers, priority, connectivity, simplicity, clear statement; plus carry-overs that failed review/tests/approval). No sprint ceremony / story points.

claim: Legacy free boards MUST tolerate out-of-vocab status on unchanged-status edits and recovery; only setting a NEW invalid status is rejected
Group delivery roll-up MUST use not_started if all/none, done only if all done, done_with_defects if all terminal with a defect, else in_progress
quote:
// Roll up a group's per-node delivery into one status. not_started if all are (or none);
	// done only if all done; done_with_defects if all terminal with a defect; else in_progress.

claim: WorkflowEngine MUST skip re-validating an unchanged status and MUST treat unknown prior status as recovery (fresh start to any valid status)
Cascade status rewrites (supersedes/unblock/effects) MUST be system actions without FSM approve gates
quote:
// Find the active node with this NodeId across the project's boards and move it to a
	// target status chosen by `pick` (null = leave as-is). System action (no gate).

claim: Leaving Blocked manually MUST close active inbound blocks edges (history kept)
quote: Leaving Blocked manually closes the active `blocks` edges into the node (history kept).

claim: Priority is part of PlanNode SamePayload so priority changes mint a new temporal revision
quote: public override bool SamePayload(TemporalRow other) => other is PlanNode p && p.Status == Status && p.Type == Type && p.Name == Name && p.Body == Body && p.Priority == Priority;

claim: MCP tasks surface MUST include board create/adopt/set_spec/list/delete/close/reopen, methodology create/list/get/close/rules/template/guide, node_get, search, upsert, delta, workflow
quote: [McpServerTool(Name = "tasks_board_close", Title = "Close (archive) a task board", UseStructuredContent = true, OutputSchemaType = typeof(BoardClosedResult))]

claim: Simple boards MUST offer fixed status vocabulary with free all-pairs transitions while still rejecting out-of-vocab statuses
quote: SIMPLE (formerly `free`; interim dogfood, not a PetBox promise). A minimal lifecycle with FREE transitions: Todo→InProgress→Done(+Cancelled), Blocked optional. Transitions are all-pairs (any valid status → any), so the generic engine yields free transitions while still rejecting an out-of-vocab status.

claim: Simple is interim dogfood and not framed as a PetBox promise in preset comments, yet it is the default board kind and a builtin template — treat as shipped capability with lower product confidence
quote: // SIMPLE (formerly `free`; interim dogfood, not a PetBox promise). A minimal lifecycle

claim: Agent process-contract items (worktree, finish=branch+commit+push) are repo agent policy, not PetBox Tasks product promises; agent ceiling Review is dogfood policy mirrored by RequiresApproval without EnforceApproval on presets
quote: **An agent never sets the final `Done` itself — its ceiling is `Review`.** The maintainer confirms.

claim: Doc methodology delivery text mentions feature/chore as implementing tasks while code RequiredTypes is feature only — code is product authority
quote: - no implementing (`feature`/`chore`) tasks, or not all Done → **not started / in progress**;

claim: Empty type on single-FSM kinds MUST be materialised to the declared default type at write time so store/UI agree with resolution
Board delete MUST purge orphaned FTS/vector search docs for that board so deleted boards cannot keep matching queries
ITasksService MUST be the single domain door for boards/nodes/workflow/delivery so adapters do not re-implement FSM or store rules
quote: The single entry point to the Tasks module for every caller (MCP tools, Razor pages, REST). It owns all reads/writes of the task store plus the domain rules (spec validation, workflow application, blockers, delivery roll-up, FSM effects), so those rules live in exactly one place instead of being re-implemented by each surface.

claim: Classic boards MUST free-move among open statuses, require reason only into Duplicate, reopen terminals to Todo, and MUST NOT carry quartet singleton/auto-wire
quote: CLASSIC (spec preset-classic) — a single-kind status model at the level of the GitHub/Jira/Linear defaults... Transitions are FREE among the OPEN statuses... terminals are reached EXPLICITLY, with a reason required only INTO Duplicate... and a closed node reopens to Todo... No approval gates anywhere, no link constraints, no effects, no checklists, free-form tags — and NO quartet semantics (no singleton rule, no auto-wire), same as `simple`.
